title: oxytropis lapponica var. humifusa
intro: Oxytropis lapponica var. humifusa, also known as the Laponic or Alpine Milk Vetch, is a perennial legume plant native to the Arctic tundra and mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Oxytropis lapponica var. humifusa include its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are made by steeping the dried flowers, leaves, or roots in hot water.
